news_items.title: England XI vs Panama: Confirmed team news, predicted lineup, injury latest for World Cup | The Standard
news_items.author: Dom Smith
news_items.text:
England XI vs Panama: Confirmed team news, predicted lineup, injury latest for World Cup | The Standard

Sport| Football

# England XI vs Panama: Confirmed team news, predicted lineup, injury latest for World Cup

Bukayo Saka and Marcus Rashford pushing for starts as Declan Rice allay fitness fears

Dom Smith in New York

1 minute ago

COMMENTS

Thomas Tuchel will be forced into a change at right-back for England’s meeting with Panama after Reece James was ruled out.

James has a hamstring injury that forced him to miss training on both Thursday and Friday. He has not travelled with the squad and is considered a doubt for England’s last-32 match as well.

The Chelsea captain has a history of injury and a hamstring issue has now disrupted his World Cup.

Djed Spence, Jarell Quansah and Ezri Konsa are the likeliest candidates to stand in for James against Panama at the MetLife Stadium on Saturday.

Tuchel lost the chance to fully rotate against Panama after England were held to the goalless draw by Ghana.

Tuchel had been expected to completely freshen up his team against Panama if England were already confirmed as winners of Group L, but Saturday’s match in New York must now take on

# England XI vs Panama: Confirmed team news, predicted lineup, injury latest for World Cup

Bukayo Saka and Marcus Rashford pushing for starts as Declan Rice allay fitness fears

Dom Smith in New York

1 minute ago

COMMENTS

Thomas Tuchel will be forced into a change at right-back for England’s meeting with Panama after Reece James was ruled out.

James has a hamstring injury that forced him to miss training on both Thursday and Friday. He has not travelled with the squad and is considered a doubt for England’s last-32 match as well.

The Chelsea captain has a history of injury and a hamstring issue has now disrupted his World Cup.

Djed Spence, Jarell Quansah and Ezri Konsa are the likeliest candidates to stand in for James against Panama at the MetLife Stadium on Saturday.

Tuchel lost the chance to fully rotate against Panama after England were held to the goalless draw by Ghana.

Tuchel had been expected to completely freshen up his team against Panama if England were already confirmed as winners of Group L, but Saturday’s match in New York must now take on

news_items.title: Panama v England | World Cup 2026 team news and predicted line-ups | Radio Times
news_items.author: Ned Holmes
news_items.text:
Panama v England | World Cup 2026 team news and predicted line-ups | Radio Times

Ahead of the World Cup, England will have hoped to be in a position to rest players for their final Group L game against Panama.

That is no longer an option after the Three Lions struggled to a goalless draw against Ghana on Tuesday. Thomas Tuchel's side are still top of the table but have work to do to make sure they progress to the knock-out stages as group winners.

England made two changes between their first two games, switching things around at the back, but more may well be in store for the game against Panama.

Read more football features: Best players in the world| Best players of all time| Live football on TV today

Though it won't be full rest and rotation, Tuchel could shuffle his pack – for both tactical and fitness reasons.

So what is the latest team news ahead of Saturday's game? And how could both sides line up?

Radio Times brings you the predicted team news for Panama v England at World Cup 2026.

#### Stay on top of the biggest World Cup ever and catch all the moments that matter with the Radio Times wall chart

https://www.radiotimes.com/tv/sport/football/world-cup-2026-wall-char

news_items.title: World Cup 2026: Reece James and Declan Rice injury concerns for England before Panama game - BBC Sport
news_items.text:
World Cup 2026: Reece James and Declan Rice injury concerns for England before Panama game - BBC Sport

Getty Images

Declan Rice and Reece James have started both of England's games at the World Cup

Senior football correspondent in Kansas City

25 June 2026

Reece James missed training with England on Thursday which has thrown his fitness into doubt for the Three Lions' final Group L game against Panama.

The 26-year-old right-back suffered a hamstring issue in the goalless draw with Ghana on Tuesday and did not train with his team-mates in England's penultimate session before Saturday's game (22:00 BST).

It now remains to be seen if the Chelsea defender is fit enough to start or be an option from the bench in the final group game at New York New Jersey Stadium.

James is a key member of the starting XI under manager Thomas Tuchel and has been selected in both World Cup games so far.

Declan Rice, who left Boston Stadium after the Ghana draw with strapping on his left calf, was also absent from Thursday's session at England's training base in Kansas City.

But sources indicate the 27-year-old midfielder's fitness is less of a concern.

It remains to be seen if Tuchel is willing

{"title": "England XI vs Panama: Confirmed team news, predicted lineup, injury latest for World Cup | The Standard", "url": "https://www.standard.co.uk/sport/football/england-xi-vs-panama-confirmed-team-news-predicted-lineup-injury-latest-world-cup-2026-b1287442.html", "publishedDate": "2026-06-26T22:43:29.000Z", "author": "Dom Smith", "text": "England XI vs Panama: Confirmed team news, predicted lineup, injury latest for World Cup | The Standard

Sport| Football

# England XI vs Panama: Confirmed team news, predicted lineup, injury latest for World Cup

Bukayo Saka and Marcus Rashford pushing for starts as Declan Rice allay fitness fears

Dom Smith in New York

1 minute ago

COMMENTS

Thomas Tuchel will be forced into a change at right-back for England’s meeting with Panama after Reece James was ruled out.

James has a hamstring injury that forced him to miss training on both Thursday and Friday. He has not travelled with the squad and is considered a doubt for England’s last-32 match as well.

The Chelsea captain has a history of injury and a hamstring issue has now disrupted his World Cup.

Djed Spence, Jarell Quansah and Ezri Konsa are the likeliest candidates to stand in for James against Panama at the MetLife Stadium on Saturday.

Tuchel lost the chance to fully rotate against Panama after England were held to the goalless draw by Ghana.

Tuchel had been expected to completely freshen up his team against Panama if England were already confirmed as winners of Group L, but Saturday’s match in New York must now take on"}
{"title": "Panama v England | World Cup 2026 team news and predicted line-ups | Radio Times", "url": "https://www.radiotimes.com/tv/sport/football/panama-england-world-cup-2026-team-news/", "publishedDate": "2026-06-26T15:48:34.865Z", "author": "Ned Holmes", "text": "Panama v England | World Cup 2026 team news and predicted line-ups | Radio Times

Ahead of the World Cup, England will have hoped to be in a position to rest players for their final Group L game against Panama.

That is no longer an option after the Three Lions struggled to a goalless draw against Ghana on Tuesday. Thomas Tuchel's side are still top of the table but have work to do to make sure they progress to the knock-out stages as group winners.

England made two changes between their first two games, switching things around at the back, but more may well be in store for the game against Panama.

Read more football features: Best players in the world| Best players of all time| Live football on TV today

Though it won't be full rest and rotation, Tuchel could shuffle his pack – for both tactical and fitness reasons.

So what is the latest team news ahead of Saturday's game? And how could both sides line up?

Radio Times brings you the predicted team news for Panama v England at World Cup 2026.

#### Stay on top of the biggest World Cup ever and catch all the moments that matter with the Radio Times wall chart

https://www.radiotimes.com/tv/sport/football/world-cup-2026-wall-char"}
{"title": "World Cup 2026: Reece James and Declan Rice injury concerns for England before Panama game - BBC Sport", "url": "https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/articles/cp8rywj8rnpo", "publishedDate": "2026-06-25T21:19:06.000Z", "author": null, "text": "World Cup 2026: Reece James and Declan Rice injury concerns for England before Panama game - BBC Sport

Getty Images

Declan Rice and Reece James have started both of England's games at the World Cup

Senior football correspondent in Kansas City

25 June 2026

Reece James missed training with England on Thursday which has thrown his fitness into doubt for the Three Lions' final Group L game against Panama.

The 26-year-old right-back suffered a hamstring issue in the goalless draw with Ghana on Tuesday and did not train with his team-mates in England's penultimate session before Saturday's game (22:00 BST).

It now remains to be seen if the Chelsea defender is fit enough to start or be an option from the bench in the final group game at New York New Jersey Stadium.

James is a key member of the starting XI under manager Thomas Tuchel and has been selected in both World Cup games so far.

Declan Rice, who left Boston Stadium after the Ghana draw with strapping on his left calf, was also absent from Thursday's session at England's training base in Kansas City.

But sources indicate the 27-year-old midfielder's fitness is less of a concern.

It remains to be seen if Tuchel is willing "}
